ai_strategy_lead.name: Carly Ries
ai_strategy_lead.title: Vice Provost of Online Learning (Nevada Online); key PACK AI academic lead for AI certificate and online curriculum

documents.title: PACK AI - Pioneering Artificial Intelligence in Higher Education (institutional AI hub)
University of Nevada, Reno launches artificial intelligence initiative, PACK AI (announcement)
AI Technology Usage Guidelines and Procedures (PACK AI)

ai_strategy.summary: University of Nevada-Reno's AI strategy is anchored by PACK AI, a president-sponsored institutional initiative launched August 13, 2025 that integrates AI into teaching, research, and business operations under a coordinated hub at unr.edu/ai. President Brian Sandoval frames PACK AI as 'our next institutional imperative,' and the strategy combines a required AI learning module for new students, Microsoft Copilot and Apple Intelligence access through the Digital Wolf Pack device program, a three-course Nevada Online generative AI certificate, faculty workshops through the Advancements in Teaching Excellence Program, and a campus-wide AI working group setting governance and policy. At the system level, the Nevada System of Higher Education (NSHE) Emerging Technology Advisory Group (ETAG) shapes cross-institution best practices.

key_provisions: Users must not share sensitive, confidential, or regulated data (FERPA, GLBA, HIPAA, PCI) with AI tools
All AI-generated content requires human review for accuracy, bias, and copyright
Any new AI tool requires a vendor risk management assessment through OIT Compliance
Microsoft Copilot is the officially licensed/recommended enterprise AI tool
Faculty must include an AI statement in the syllabus (permitted/selective/prohibited options) and discuss it day one
Adherence to UAM 6,502 Academic Integrity and disclosure of AI use in academic settings
New students must complete a required introduction-to-AI module during NevadaFIT covering use, ethics, and integrity
Policy is periodically reviewed with stakeholder feedback and regulatory updates
ai_policy.summary: University of Nevada-Reno operates a comprehensive, documented AI policy stack combining an OIT Artificial Intelligence Usage Policy that governs all AI use on university-owned resources with PACK AI-authored technology usage procedures, student guidelines, academic integrity guidance, and faculty syllabus statement templates. The stance is permissive with significant guardrails: AI is encouraged where it serves learning and research, Microsoft Copilot is the endorsed enterprise tool, but data classification, vendor vetting through OIT Compliance, and mandatory syllabus-level disclosure are required. All incoming students complete a mandatory AI literacy module via NevadaFIT.

bodies.name: PACK AI Working Group
bodies.scope: Campus-wide faculty/staff group studying AI policies, identifying best practices, recommending adoption strategies, and developing university-wide governance structures for academic, administrative, and research uses

bodies.name: Office of Information Technology (OIT) Compliance
bodies.scope: Administers vendor risk management assessments for proposed AI tools; oversees implementation and enforcement of AI Usage Policy; maintains data classification and FERPA/HIPAA/PCI compliance guardrails

bodies.name: Advancements in Teaching Excellence Program
bodies.scope: Faculty development arm delivering generative AI workshops (assignments, tutoring, ethics) and syllabus guidance under the PACK AI umbrella

bodies.name: Nevada System of Higher Education Emerging Technology Advisory Group (ETAG)
bodies.scope: System-level body (chartered August 2025) providing cross-NSHE recommendations on emerging technology best practices for teaching, research, workforce development, and operational efficiency; UNR participates

governance_structure.summary: Governance is distributed but coherent: the PACK AI working group functions as UNR's de facto AI council, working alongside OIT (policy and compliance), the Advancements in Teaching Excellence Program (faculty development), Nevada Online (credential programs), and University Libraries. At the system level, NSHE's ETAG provides cross-institution alignment. UNR has not named a dedicated chief AI officer but has coalesced responsibility under the president's office with the working group and VP for IT Sasi Pillay.

initiatives.title: PACK AI institutional AI initiative launch
initiatives.description: President Sandoval launched PACK AI on August 13, 2025 as UNR's signature campus-wide AI initiative, integrating teaching, faculty development, research support, and AI-enabled business processes under a single hub at unr.edu/ai. The initiative was kicked off at a September 4-5, 2025 event featuring faculty showcases of AI in instruction.
initiatives.significance: PACK AI marks one of the most complete, presidentially sponsored AI operating systems among R1 publics: it folds policy, student tools, curriculum, faculty training, and governance into a single institutional brand.

investment.funding_source: Institutional (President's office) with partner units OIT, University Libraries, Office of Research and Innovation, Advancements in Teaching Excellence
investment.description: No dollar amount disclosed; resources are operational rather than one-time capital

collaborations.description: Student-facing tool access via Microsoft Copilot and Apple Intelligence delivered through Digital Wolf Pack devices

tools_and_vendors.name: Copilot Chat
tools_and_vendors.role: Campus-endorsed generative AI chat and productivity assistant for students and staff

tools_and_vendors.name: Apple Intelligence
tools_and_vendors.role: On-device generative AI features on university-issued iPads

initiatives.policy_implications: Establishes institution-wide baseline that AI is an expected literacy and productivity layer while requiring compliance with data-classification, vendor-vetting, and academic-integrity guardrails

initiatives.title: Required AI literacy module in NevadaFIT first-year experience
initiatives.description: Beginning Fall 2025, all new students must complete an introduction-to-AI module as part of the NevadaFIT Freshman Intensive Transition Program covering AI use, ethics, academic integrity, and UNR's AI policy.
initiatives.significance: Converts AI literacy from elective enrichment into a universal first-year graduation prerequisite, a governance pattern few R1s have operationalized at this scale.

investment.description: Operational investment in curriculum module development and NevadaFIT integration

initiatives.policy_implications: Makes baseline AI literacy a shared floor for all matriculants and links curriculum to UAM 6,502 academic integrity

initiatives.title: Three-course Nevada Online generative AI certificate
initiatives.description: Nevada Online, led by Vice Provost Carly Ries, is developing a three-course online certificate covering introduction to AI, AI ethics (taught by philosophy professor Jason Fisette), and AI applications, open to all UNR students and modeled on the University of Florida's nationally known certificate.
initiatives.significance: Extends AI literacy beyond the freshman module into a credentialed pathway and mirrors top-of-field peer programs, signaling UNR is investing in scalable workforce credentials rather than one-off courses.

investment.description: Curriculum development investment; no dollar figure disclosed

collaborations.description: Certificate intentionally mirrors the University of Florida's successful AI certificate program

initiatives.policy_implications: Normalizes ethics as a required element of credentialed AI learning at UNR

initiatives.title: OIT Artificial Intelligence Usage Policy
initiatives.description: The Office of Information Technology's AI Usage Policy sets enforceable guardrails for all AI use on university-owned or managed computing resources, covering data classification (FERPA/GLBA/HIPAA/PCI), vendor risk management, academic integrity (UAM 6,502), human review of AI outputs, and periodic policy review.
initiatives.significance: One of the more operationally detailed public-university AI usage policies, binding employees, students, contractors, vendors, third parties, and guests to a single framework.

investment.description: Policy development and enforcement cost only

tools_and_vendors.name: Copilot
tools_and_vendors.role: Only officially licensed enterprise AI tool at UNR

initiatives.policy_implications: Sets baseline enforceable expectations for vendor assessment and data privacy across all AI use

initiatives.title: Digital Wolf Pack Initiative with AI-capable devices
initiatives.description: All incoming full-time freshmen and transfer students receive an Apple iPad Air or Microsoft Surface Pro preloaded with Microsoft Copilot and Apple Intelligence via the Digital Wolf Pack Initiative, closing the AI-tool access gap across the incoming cohort.
initiatives.significance: Goes beyond licensing to physical hardware equity, ensuring every first-year student has identical AI tool access regardless of income - a distinctive equity move among R1s.

investment.description: Multi-million-dollar device program with Copilot and Apple Intelligence added in 2025; exact AI uplift not itemized

collaborations.description: Device and licensed AI feature delivery

tools_and_vendors.name: iPad Air
tools_and_vendors.role: Student device platform with Apple Intelligence AI features

tools_and_vendors.name: Surface Pro
tools_and_vendors.role: Alternative student device platform with Copilot

initiatives.policy_implications: Equity-first AI access aligned with university acceptable use policy

initiatives.title: NSF AI Institute for Dynamic Systems (partnership)
initiatives.description: UNR Mechanical Engineering professors Aditya Nair and Floris van Breugel are founding members of the NSF-funded $20 million AI Institute for Dynamic Systems, led by the University of Washington with partners including Columbia, Harvard, UH-Manoa, Montana State, Boise State, UA-Anchorage, and Portland State. The institute focuses on physics-informed AI for dynamic systems.
initiatives.significance: Embeds UNR in a top-tier NSF AI Institute, placing the university in a national consortium on physics-informed machine learning.

investment.funding_source: National Science Foundation (institute total; UNR receives sub-award)
investment.description: Five-year $20M NSF AI Institute program; UNR is one of 9 partner institutions

initiatives.title: IFML / UT-Austin medical AI partnership and $1M sub-award
initiatives.description: Assistant Professor Ankita Shukla and Foundation Professor George Bebis received a $1 million sub-award (announced March 6, 2026) from the NSF-funded Institute for Foundations of Machine Learning (IFML) at UT-Austin to develop robust, reliable medical AI in three domains: breast cancer imaging, clinical text, and sleep-disorder diagnosis, with K-12 teacher outreach.
initiatives.significance: Expands UNR's AI research portfolio into safety-critical medical AI and connects the institution to one of the major NSF ML institutes at UT-Austin.

investment.funding_source: NSF via IFML at UT-Austin (sub-award to UNR)
investment.description: Five-year sub-award supporting medical AI robustness and fairness research with middle/high school outreach

partners: University of Texas at Austin / Institute for Foundations of Machine Learning (IFML)
collaborations.description: UT-Austin IFML is the NSF parent institute; UNR leads medical AI subprojects with clinical and K-12 partners

initiatives.title: Bridge to Research in AI and Neurocomputing (BRAIN) Core
initiatives.description: A University Core Facility within the Center for Integrative Neuroscience providing training, technical support, and equipment in machine learning/AI, statistical analysis, and virtual/augmented reality for UNR researchers across disciplines.
initiatives.significance: Lowers the barrier for non-CS faculty to incorporate ML/AI methods into their research, signaling AI becoming a shared scientific infrastructure rather than a CS-only specialty.

investment.description: Core facility fees and institutional contribution

initiatives.title: Pronghorn HPC cluster expansion with NSF CC* NvBAISEC award
initiatives.description: UNR expanded its Pronghorn high-performance computing cluster with a 10-node NVIDIA A100 GPU partition via the NSF CC* 'Nevada Bridge to AI-enabled Scientific & Engineering Computing' (NvBAISEC) award (#2201599), creating a central AI/ML compute facility for campus researchers. Pronghorn is hosted at the Switch Citadel data center via a $3.4M Switch services donation.
initiatives.significance: Provides the GPU backbone for Reno's growing AI research portfolio and illustrates a public-private infrastructure model tying university HPC to Nevada's commercial data-center ecosystem.

investment.funding_source: Switch (in-kind data-center donation) plus NSF CC* program (A100 GPU cluster)
investment.description: $3.4M in Switch data-center services for Pronghorn hosting; NSF CC* award funding for A100 GPU expansion

collaborations.description: Industry hosting and federal research infrastructure funding

tools_and_vendors.name: A100 GPUs
tools_and_vendors.role: GPU compute nodes for AI/ML workloads

tools_and_vendors.name: Citadel Data Center
tools_and_vendors.role: Hosting and power for Pronghorn cluster

initiatives.title: Intelligent Mobility Initiative and Nevada Autonomous Living Labs
initiatives.description: Led by Civil & Environmental Engineering Professor Hao Xu through the Nevada Center for Applied Research, the Intelligent Mobility Initiative is a multi-year connected-and-autonomous-vehicle Living Lab spanning Reno, Sparks, and Carson City, using an autonomous Lincoln MKZ testbed, LiDAR, DSRC, traffic signals, and blockchain/IoT (Filament partnership) for real-world AV research.
initiatives.significance: A rare state-and-city-scale autonomous vehicle Living Lab anchored at an R1; it demonstrates how regional transportation infrastructure can become applied AI research infrastructure.

investment.funding_source: Nevada Governor's Office of Economic Development, industry partners, federal grants
investment.description: Coalition-funded initiative via Nevada Center for Applied Research

partners: Nevada Governor's Office of Economic Development (GOED)

initiatives.policy_implications: Informs Nevada state transportation AI regulation through academic and judicial research partners

initiatives.title: Faculty development workshops in generative AI teaching
initiatives.description: The Advancements in Teaching Excellence Program, directed by Dr. Sarah Cummings, delivers workshops to UNR faculty on incorporating generative AI into courses, assignments, tutoring, and ethics, with experienced faculty peers leading the sessions and sharing best practices.
initiatives.significance: Institutionalizes faculty AI upskilling rather than leaving it to individual colleges or departments; critical enabler of the required student AI literacy module.

investment.description: Operating-cost investment in faculty workshops and digital learning resources

initiatives.policy_implications: Supports the university's syllabus-level AI policy by training faculty to author credible AI statements

initiatives.title: Robotics M.S. degree program
initiatives.description: UNR's College of Engineering launched a multidisciplinary Master's of Science in Robotics with thesis and courses-only options, covering kinematics, dynamics, sensing, actuation, computation, human-robot interaction, and autonomous systems.
initiatives.significance: Adds a graduate credential pathway into applied AI / robotics, complementing the existing CSE M.S./Ph.D. and robotics minor; important for workforce pipeline with regional autonomous vehicle and aerospace industries.

initiatives.title: CREDIT+ center for AI-enabled mission-critical data analysis
initiatives.description: Electrical Engineering Associate Professor Hao Xu secured a $1 million Department of Defense grant in Fall 2024 to establish the CREDIT+ center (Advancing Data Analysis for Mission-Critical Applications in the era of Artificial Intelligence).
initiatives.significance: Deepens UNR's federal AI research portfolio into defense-relevant data analysis and mission-critical reliability.

investment.description: DoD grant to establish CREDIT+ center

initiatives.title: NSF CAREER award: Physics-Informed Machine Learning for Geostructural Systems
initiatives.description: Civil Engineering Assistant Professor Elnaz Seylabi won a 5-year, $608,000 NSF CAREER award for 'A Physics-Informed Machine Learning (PIML) Framework for Surrogate Modeling of Geostructural Systems.'
initiatives.significance: Illustrates how UNR faculty are winning competitive NSF AI/ML awards outside the traditional CS disciplines and applying ML to infrastructure resilience.

investment.description: 5-year Faculty Early Career Development Program award

computing_infrastructure.summary: Pronghorn is UNR's primary HPC cluster, hosted at Switch's Citadel Campus under a $3.4M in-kind Switch donation. The base configuration provides 108 CPU nodes with 3,456 cores and 24.8 TiB RAM, a GPU partition with 44 NVIDIA Tesla P100 GPUs, and a visualization partition with 3 NVIDIA Tesla V100 GPUs, plus 2 PiB IBM Spectrum Scale storage, all interconnected over 100 Gb/s Intel Omni-Path. An NSF CC* award (NvBAISEC, #2201599) added a 10-node NVIDIA A100 GPU cluster expressly to support campus AI/ML workloads. The BRAIN Core provides additional ML/AI services for researchers.

equity_and_access_initiatives.title: Digital Wolf Pack Initiative AI tool access
equity_and_access_initiatives.description: Every incoming full-time freshman and transfer student receives a university-issued iPad Air or Surface Pro preloaded with Microsoft Copilot Chat and Apple Intelligence, closing the AI tool access gap across income levels.

equity_and_access_initiatives.title: K-12 AI education outreach via IFML medical AI partnership
equity_and_access_initiatives.description: The UT-Austin IFML sub-award includes partnering with regional middle and high school teachers to integrate AI education into K-12 curricula, extending UNR's AI mission into underserved community schools.

equity_and_access_initiatives.title: UNR Digital Accessibility (WCAG 2.1 AA) policy
equity_and_access_initiatives.description: UNR's ICT accessibility policy holds AI-infused digital content to WCAG 2.1 Level AA and requires equivalent alternatives when technical infeasibility applies.

regulatory_context.summary: Nevada enacted AB 406 in the 2025 legislative session, prohibiting AI use by K-12 counselors, psychologists, and social workers (except administrative tasks) and directing the Nevada Department of Education to guide responsible AI use in student mental health services. The Nevada Department of Education also published 'Nevada's STELLAR Pathway to AI Teaching and Learning' in April 2025. At the higher-ed system level, NSHE established the Emerging Technology Advisory Group (ETAG) under a charter dated August 24, 2025 to align AI best practices across its institutions; UNR actively participates and contributed to the system-wide 'Artificial Intelligence at NSHE August 2025' document. There is no Nevada executive order specifically governing higher-education AI use.

narrative_summary: The University of Nevada-Reno has become one of the more strategically coherent R1 AI adopters in the Mountain West. Under President Brian Sandoval, the August 2025 launch of PACK AI - branded as the university's 'next institutional imperative' - coordinates previously distributed efforts into a single campus-wide operating system spanning policy, curriculum, infrastructure, and faculty development. On the student-facing side, PACK AI mandates an AI literacy module in the NevadaFIT freshman orientation, delivers Microsoft Copilot Chat and Apple Intelligence to every incoming freshman and transfer student through the Digital Wolf Pack device program, and is spinning up a three-course Nevada Online generative AI certificate modeled on the University of Florida. Governance is anchored by a campus-wide PACK AI working group, the Office of Information Technology's AI Usage Policy (which requires vendor risk assessments, data-classification compliance, and human review of AI outputs), faculty syllabus statement templates, and alignment with NSHE's Emerging Technology Advisory Group. On the research side, UNR is a founding partner in the NSF AI Institute for Dynamic Systems ($20M), has a new $1M sub-award from UT-Austin's IFML for robust medical AI, a $1M DoD-funded CREDIT+ center, and a $608K NSF CAREER award on physics-informed ML. The Pronghorn HPC cluster, hosted at Switch's Citadel data center, runs the NSF CC*-funded NvBAISEC A100 GPU partition and is complemented by the BRAIN Core ML/AI shared facility. The Nevada Center for Applied Research's Intelligent Mobility and Nevada Autonomous programs make Reno-Sparks-Carson City a Living Lab for connected and autonomous vehicles in partnership with Filament, RTC, and GOED. Equity is built into device distribution rather than grafted on, and the university aligns with state-level AI education guidance (STELLAR, AB 406) even where those laws target K-12. Gaps include no named chief AI officer, no dedicated AI ethics center, and no publicly disclosed totals for PACK AI funding.
